name: bully
title: bully
summary: Implementation of the WPS brute force attack, written in C
description:
Implementation of the WPS brute force attack, written in C
Bully is a new implementation of the WPS brute force attack, written in C. It
is conceptually identical to other programs, in that it exploits the (now well
known) design flaw in the WPS specification. It has several advantages over
the original reaver code. These include fewer dependencies, improved memory
and cpu performance, correct handling of endianness, and a more robust set of
options.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bully because Implementation of the WPS brute force attack, written in C.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bully as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bully [options] [args]` as documented upstream for bully.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bully with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bully --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
